decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a margin of ~16°C. Following the optimization history suggestion to shift strategy due to diminishing returns on material-only tuning, I will apply a coordinated geometry change to the heat spreader. Increasing both width and height will expand the thermal cross-section and lateral spreading area, offering a new path to reduce the chip peak temperature.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader width within bounds increases the lateral area available for heat dissipation towards the side boundaries.
Increasing the spreader height adds vertical thermal mass and cross-section, improving conduction away from the chip interface.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the upper domain
Decreased thermal resistance between chip and boundary conditions
risk_notes: Approaching geometric limits (width=1.0) may limit future geometry-based improvements
Diminishing returns if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions
Minor increase in total material volume (if cost were a factor)
